title: Subject Analysis: Cross-Term/Grade Averages & Computer Science Marks Fix

requirements.summary: Calculate and display per-grade subject averages across all terms in Subject Analysis
acceptanceCriteria: For each grade, each subject shows an average percentage computed over all terms that have data for that subject in that grade.
The per-grade average is clearly labeled (e.g., 'Grade Average across all terms').
The average updates correctly when new marks entries are added.

file_operations.description: Add a new function `calculatePerGradeSubjectAverages` that accepts all academic entries and computes per-grade subject averages across all terms. The function should group entries by grade, then for each grade compute the average percentage for each subject that has data across all terms in that grade. Return a structure mapping grade to subject to average percentage.

Update the SubjectAnalysisView component to display per-grade subject averages alongside or below the existing per-term breakdown. When a specific grade is selected in the filter, call the new `calculatePerGradeSubjectAverages` function from subjectAnalysis.ts and render a clearly labeled section (e.g., 'Grade X Average across all terms') showing each subject's average percentage for that grade. Ensure the display updates when new marks are added by leveraging React Query's cache invalidation.

requirements.summary: Calculate and display overall subject averages across all grades in Subject Analysis
acceptanceCriteria: A summary section or row in Subject Analysis shows each subject's average percentage computed across all grades and all terms.
This overall average is clearly labeled (e.g., 'Overall Average across all grades').
Only subjects with at least one data entry contribute to the average.

file_operations.description: Add a new function `calculateOverallSubjectAverages` that accepts all academic entries and computes each subject's average percentage across all grades and all terms. For each subject, aggregate all available marks and max marks entries, compute the overall percentage, and return a structure mapping subject to overall average percentage. Only include subjects with at least one data entry.

Update the SubjectAnalysisView component to display an overall subject average summary section. Call the new `calculateOverallSubjectAverages` function from subjectAnalysis.ts and render a clearly labeled section (e.g., 'Overall Average across all grades') showing each subject's average percentage computed across all grades and terms. Position this section prominently (e.g., at the top or as a summary card) so users can see cross-grade trends at a glance.

requirements.summary: Update Computer Science total marks configuration to 20 marks for Grades 1-7 and 30 marks for Grade 8
acceptanceCriteria: In frontend/src/lib/maxMarks.ts (or equivalent), Computer Science max marks is set to 20 for Grades 1–7.
In frontend/src/lib/maxMarks.ts (or equivalent), Computer Science max marks is set to 30 for Grade 8.
Subject Analysis percentage for Computer Science correctly reflects 20-mark total for Grades 1–7 and 30-mark total for Grade 8.
AddMarksForm enforces the correct max marks for Computer Science based on the selected grade.
No other subjects or grades are affected by this change.

file_operations.description: Update the Computer Science max marks configuration so that Grades 1–7 have a total of 20 marks, and Grade 8 has a total of 30 marks. Modify the `getMaxMarks` or equivalent helper function to return 20 for computerMaxMarks when grade is 1–7, and 30 when grade is 8. Ensure no other subjects or grades are affected. Verify that all percentage calculation paths (e.g., in Subject Analysis, Progress, Combined %, etc.) use this grade-specific max marks value for Computer Science.

Update the AddMarksForm component to enforce the correct Computer Science max marks based on the selected grade. When the grade is 1–7, set computerMaxMarks to 20; when the grade is 8, set computerMaxMarks to 30. Ensure the form validation and input fields reflect these grade-specific limits. This ensures users cannot enter marks exceeding the correct total for Computer Science in each grade.
